title: Go Messaging Observability, Capacity, and Alarms · Execution Trace: Message Flow Telemetry
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must propagate trace context, correlate publish-to-effect evidence, monitor Go runtime and broker signals, and shed load on alarms; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the message flow telemetry increment in a low-cardinality messaging telemetry and capacity plan…
objectives: Instrument publish, confirm, return, route, deliver, process, ack, retry, dead-letter, and effect phases with bounded identities and causal links; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Defend tenant topology, message size, route, rate, queue count, headers, permissions, and noisy-neighbor budgets without trusting publisher metadata; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Message Flow Telemetry · Theory Message Flow Telemetry
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go messaging team handles case 7259 during stream-versus-queue architecture board. Build deterministic Go evidence to combine backlog, age, alarm, consumer, and bounded-capacity evidence; bound payload, queue, confirm-ledger, and handler bytes; then remove the parking binding and expose the remediation gap. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Instrument publish, confirm, return, route, deliver, process, ack, retry, dead-letter, and effect phases with bounded identities and causal links; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
